---
title: "Results (generated section)"
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
# Anchor on the git root: this report is its own Quarto project.
source(file.path(rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::is_git_root), "R", "01_setup.R"))
clean <- get_clean_consumer_data()

# ---- Fit the shared models (defined once in R/functions/analysis.R) -------
fit_main <- fit_main_model(clean)
med      <- fit_mediation_models(clean)
fit_mod  <- fit_moderation_model(clean)

tidy_main <- broom::tidy(fit_main, conf.int = TRUE)
b_comb <- tidy_main$estimate[tidy_main$term == "conditioncombined_frame"]
p_comb <- tidy_main$p.value[tidy_main$term == "conditioncombined_frame"]

# ---- Save publication figures (vector PDF) into generated/figures/ --------
fig_dir <- project_path("renders", "manuscript", "generated", "figures")
dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_01_pi_by_condition.pdf"),
                plot_pi_by_condition(clean), width = 7, height = 4)
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_02_mediation_scatter.pdf"),
                plot_mediation_scatter(clean), width = 7, height = 4)
ggplot2::ggsave(file.path(fig_dir, "fig_03_moderation.pdf"),
                plot_moderation(clean), width = 7, height = 4)

# ---- Save the model table (LaTeX) into generated/tabs/ --------------------
tab_dir <- project_path("renders", "manuscript", "generated", "tabs")
dir.create(tab_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
tidy_models <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  total       = broom::tidy(med$total, conf.int = TRUE),
  mediator    = broom::tidy(med$a,     conf.int = TRUE),
  outcome_w_m = broom::tidy(med$b,     conf.int = TRUE),
  moderation  = broom::tidy(fit_mod,   conf.int = TRUE),
  .id = "model"
)
tidy_models |>
  dplyr::transmute(
    Model = model, Term = term,
    Estimate = round(estimate, 2), SE = round(std.error, 2),
    `CI low` = round(conf.low, 2), `CI high` = round(conf.high, 2),
    p = round(p.value, 3)
  ) |>
  knitr::kable(format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, longtable = FALSE,
               caption = "Model estimates with 95\\% confidence intervals.",
               label = "models") |>
  writeLines(file.path(tab_dir, "model_table.tex"))
```

```{=latex}
% GENERATED FROM reports/manuscript/results.qmd — DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.
% Edit the prose in the .qmd, then: quarto render reports/manuscript
```

\section{Results}

\subsection{Main effect}

Relative to the control frame, the combined frame raised purchase intention by
`r sprintf("%.2f", b_comb)` points on the seven-point scale
($p = `r sprintf("%.3f", p_comb)`$). Figure~\ref{fig:main} shows the full
pattern across conditions.

\begin{figure}[h]
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.85\textwidth]{generated/figures/fig_01_pi_by_condition.pdf}
  \caption{Purchase intention by condition. Red points are condition means with 95\% confidence intervals.}
  \label{fig:main}
\end{figure}

\subsection{Mediation through perceived value}

The frames also shifted perceived value upward, and perceived value strongly
predicted purchase intention (Figure~\ref{fig:mediation}); adding it to the
outcome model attenuated the condition coefficients, the signature of an
indirect path.

\begin{figure}[h]
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.85\textwidth]{generated/figures/fig_02_mediation_scatter.pdf}
  \caption{Perceived value predicts purchase intention; the frames shift perceived value upward, producing an indirect path.}
  \label{fig:mediation}
\end{figure}

\subsection{Moderation by price sensitivity}

The combined-frame boost was attenuated among the most price-sensitive
respondents (Figure~\ref{fig:moderation}).

\begin{figure}[h]
  \centering
  \includegraphics[width=0.85\textwidth]{generated/figures/fig_03_moderation.pdf}
  \caption{Condition $\times$ price sensitivity: the combined-frame boost shrinks for high-price-sensitive respondents.}
  \label{fig:moderation}
\end{figure}

\subsection{Model estimates}

Table~\ref{tab:models} collects the coefficients behind the figures above. It is
generated by \texttt{reports/manuscript/results.qmd} and written into
\texttt{generated/tabs/}, so the paper's numbers track the analysis exactly.

\input{generated/tabs/model_table.tex}
